_meta.description: Payer intelligence for the requested service (MRI lumbar spine w/o contrast, CPT 72148). Seeded from the Praxigen coverage API (source-grounded payer policy criteria); refreshed by scripts/refresh_payer_intel.py on the cron schedule in ops/CRON.md. Criteria text is summarized from cited public sources, never fabricated.
predictive_variables: rbm_delegate (eviCore/Carelon routing predicts criteria family + turnaround)
denial_pattern (insufficient conservative-therapy evidence is the modal denial for this family)

payers.criteriaSummary: MRI appropriate when: (a) pain persisted/progressed despite >=6 weeks conservative management and patient is a surgical/interventional candidate or diagnostic uncertainty remains; (b) radiculopathy >6 weeks with failed conservative management; or (c) red flags (cauda equina, malignancy, infection, severe/progressive neuro deficit). Document conservative-care trial (type and duration) and red-flag findings.
sources: ACR Appropriateness Criteria Low Back Pain 2021 Update (jacr.org)

payers.criteriaSummary: MRI appropriate when persistent/progressive symptoms remain after >=6 weeks conservative management in a surgical/interventional candidate, radiculopathy >6 weeks despite conservative care, or red flags present. Document conservative-care type/duration and red-flag findings.
sources: ACR Appropriateness Criteria Low Back Pain 2021 Update (jacr.org)

payers.criteriaFamily: Part B medical necessity (AUC program paused; 42 CFR 414.94 rescinded 2024)
payers.criteriaSummary: Traditional Medicare FFS generally does NOT require prior authorization for outpatient MRI; covered under Part B when medically necessary. Medicare Advantage plans set their own PA rules (CMS-0057-F timelines: 7 days standard / 72h expedited).
sources: CMS Appropriate Use Criteria Program (paused; CMS MM13485)

payers.criteriaFamily: No Praxigen row for CPT 72148 yet — the nightly sync (ops/CRON.md) pulls it when published. Demo criteria C1-C5 are hand-structured from the same public ACR-tracking policy language.
